mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives equity valuations higher over the next trading month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding residential mortgage debt indicate synchronized credit and construction demand, which historically leads equity markets upward over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any 3-year rolling period, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An uptick in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly improves corporate profit margins and lifts equity prices within a one-week window.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO month-over-month changes, or the Spearman rank correlation drops below 0.05 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms robust aggregate demand, which sustains earnings revision cycles and pushes the S&P 500 higher over the following month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5 percent
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth exceeding 150,000 while core inflation remains contained below 3.0 percent signals a soft-landing environment, which reduces recession risk premiums and elevates equity valuations over a three-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 150k monthly while CPIAUCSL stays below 3.0% YoY
failure_condition: The interaction model yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient for the joint signal, or forward 60-day returns under this regime do not exceed the unconditional mean by at least 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous growth in residential mortgage debt outstanding reflects sustained household leverage and consumption financing, which historically precedes equity market rallies over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if monthly RSAFS changes show zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns, or if the out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 15-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 4.0 percent during an inflationary environment increase borrowing costs and compress equity risk premiums, systematically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional correlation between rate levels and subsequent equity returns becomes positive over a 3-year window.
